mod.fps_horizon.name: FPS Horizon
fps_horizon.config.title: FPS Horizon - Configuration
fps_horizon.config.minFps.tooltip: If average FPS drops below this value, render distance will decrease.
fps_horizon.config.maxFps.tooltip: If average FPS exceeds this value, render distance will increase.
fps_horizon.config.fpsSamples.tooltip:
How many FPS samples to average before deciding a change.
More samples = slower but more stable reaction.
fps_horizon.config.minRenderDistance.too: Minimum render distance in chunks. The mod will never go below this value.
fps_horizon.config.maxRenderDistance.too: Maximum render distance in chunks. The mod will never exceed this value.
fps_horizon.config.cooldownBajar.tooltip:
Ticks to wait after DECREASING render distance before evaluating again.
20 ticks = 1 second.
fps_horizon.config.cooldownSubir.tooltip: Ticks to wait after INCREASING render distance before evaluating again.
fps_horizon.config.nieblaActiva.tooltip: Enables or disables the distance fog that hides chunk loading during render distance changes.
fps_horizon.config.fogStartBloques.toolt:
Fixed distance in blocks where the fog begins.
0 = fog starts at the camera (Silent Hill style)
64 = fog starts at 64 blocks (recommended).
This value never changes with render distance.
fps_horizon.config.fogEnd.tooltip:
Fraction of the render distance where fog becomes fully opaque.
0.75 = opaque at 75% of the distance
0.95 = opaque near the edge (see further).
fps_horizon.config.fogCierreEnd.tooltip:
How far the fog closes when a render distance change happens.
Lower = more aggressive close, safer to hide chunk edges.
Higher = softer close, may show edges if fog speed is slow.
fps_horizon.config.fogVelocidadLerp.tool:
Animation speed of the fog transition.
0.01 = very slow
0.15 = fast.
fps_horizon.config.mostrarDebug.tooltip: Shows the render distance in the Action Bar when it changes.
fps_horizon.config.debugVerbose.tooltip: Shows average FPS, state and cooldown on every tick in the Action Bar.
fps_horizon.config.cullingActivo.tooltip: Enables distance-based culling that hides chunks and entities outside an ellipsoid shape, reducing GPU load.
fps_horizon.config.cullingVertical.toolt:
How aggressively chunks above and below are culled.
0.50x = sphere (same in all directions)
2.00x = default (recommended)
10.0x = very aggressive vertical culling

At 4 chunks+ render distance, 2.0x works well.
Below 3 chunks, try 1.25x to avoid holes.
fps_horizon.config.cullingHorizontal.too:
Extends the render ellipsoid horizontally.
0% = normal sphere
10% = slight extension (recommended)
100% = maximum horizontal extension
fps_horizon.config.cullingEntidades.tool: Applies the same culling to entities (mobs, items, etc). May improve performance in areas with many entities.
fps_horizon.config.cullingDinamico.toolt:
Automatically adjusts culling based on current render distance.
  RD 1-2: Vertical 0.50x, Horizontal 30%
  RD 3:   Vertical 0.50x, Horizontal 40%
  RD 4+:  Uses your configured values

Overrides the sliders when active for low RD values.
fps_horizon.config.cullingPerfiles.toolt:
[Experimental] Activates the culling profiles system.
When active, the vertical and horizontal sliders are disabled.
Each profile defines culling values for a specific render distance.
fps_horizon.profiles.title: FPS Horizon - Culling Profiles
fps_horizon.profiles.empty: No profiles yet. Press Add to create one.
fps_horizon.profiles.name: Profile name
